actual_coverage.reasoning: Both ask for multi-year, durable drivers of growth (yield vs cost programs vs assets/capital allocation) and how the setup compares to prior periods.
Predicted asks for percent booked by quarter (Q2–Q4) and durability of Med rebound, which addresses the back-half yield cadence supporting full-year guidance.
Directly requests percent of capacity sold, remaining inventory and booking velocity for Q3 Med, aligning with damage assessment and guidance assumptions.
Asks to quantify the Med moderation and current curve shape versus normal, which matches the need to compare booking trajectory to pre-disruption levels and across time horizons.
Predicted quantifies APD uplift and ramp/payback for Perfect Day Mexico/Costa Maya and timeline shifts, which captures ramp cadence and longer-term Western Caribbean yield potential.
Predicted discusses Cozumel/Mexico timing and mitigation but does not ask the specific construction status/resumption question.
Nearly verbatim on whether 2026 European headwinds should revert in 2027.
Directly decomposes full-year yield into like-for-like vs hardware/private destinations; aligns with regional (Europe) component of guide.
Predicted asks for operational levers (speed/routing/fuel mix) to mitigate fuel, analogous to itinerary/cost adjustments and harvesting efficiencies.
Focuses on hedge sizing/pricing philosophy and extending hedges, which addresses rolling in new hedges in a high fuel environment; partial on the NCC ex-fuel scaling threshold.
Predicted quantifies co-brand/loyalty contributions to bookings/yield, consistent with assessing implications of higher repeat/loyalty on net yield and behavior.
Predicted addresses air connectivity actions mainly for Med/Mexico, not North American airfare sensitivity for NA itineraries.

missed_actual_themes: Specific project execution update: whether construction at Perfect Day Mexico has resumed
Impact of higher North American airfares on consumer behavior for North America itineraries
overpredicted_themes: Capital allocation, buybacks, leverage targets and financing mix (ECA vs unsecured, covenants, maturity ladder)
Detailed joint venture/TUI contribution and guidance reconciliation
West Coast Mexico itinerary weakness and revenue/yield quantification
Explicit guidance 'buffer' sizing and broad EPS sensitivity tables (FX/APD beyond fuel)
Source-market mix shifts (North American vs international sourcing)
summary: Coverage was strong (10/12 actuals), with very close matches on Mediterranean booking/rebound, yield decomposition including hardware/private destinations, cost cadence and fuel-hedging, and loyalty/digital monetization. Two areas were not adequately covered: the specific construction status at Perfect Day Mexico and the impact of higher North American airfares on NA itineraries. Precision was solid (0.69), but many predictions over-indexed on capital markets/capital return and JV/TUI topics that did not surface in the holdout.
